inline_comments.message: we have a template for that, please use it (see CPS deployment).

you should also add a service account

see serviceAccount chart and https://gerrit.onap.org/r/c/oom/+/118943 which add it to cassandra

according to https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/persistent-volumes#deployments_vs_statefulsets, we may be in trouble while upgrading when using a deployment and PVC.
I don't have "simple" idea no how to resolve but it may be good to have a statefulset here

remove all of this and use:

{{ include "common.PV" . }}

remove all of this and use

{{ include "common.PVC" . }}

I would have done:
    - port: 80
      internal_port: *cont_port
      name: &port http

this  allow to do http://chartmuseum.ns/ to connect and don't bother with 8080 part (which will be only on pod)
I guess we shouldn't wait for anything here
Dont recall seeing this on most onap charts.. could you clarify criteria under which serviceAccount setting are required?
May need help with this; will address the other comments first.
Yeah, agreed. Will modified as proposed

I added below to deployment 
"serviceAccountName: {{ include "common.fullname" (dict "suffix" "read" "dot" . )}}

but got following error during install - 
Error: unable to build kubernetes objects from release manifest: error validating "": error validating data: ValidationError(Deployment.spec.template.spec.containers[0]): unknown field "serviceAccountName" in io.k8s.api.core.v1.Container

The requirements.yaml was updated to include serviceAccount dependency; not sure why this error. For now i've commented out this line.
Introduction of chartmuseum as internal repo for
could you use "shell" only script (bash is GPLv3, it can be an issue later)
see comment in values but here: {{  include "repositoryGenerator.githubRepository" . }}/{{ .Values.image }}
I would also add {{ include "common.containerSecurityContext" . | indent XXX | trim}}

why don't you use templates from secrets? (indent may not be accurate...):

{{- include "common.secret.envFromSecretFast" (dict "global" . "uid" "registrycred" "key" "login") | indent 14 }}

any reason not to add it?
I think "nothing" is sufficient

don't hardcode ghcr.io here please (it won't work in airgap mode)
create a separate commit to add ghcr.io into repositoryGenerator + onap/values.yaml (like k8s repo)

no need for "read" role here (no access to kubernetes API)
so you can remove L78 and L79

why 100 and 655533?

you're setting securityContext two times (here and L47)
can we try a bit less for requests?
then should be after a semi-colon and on the previous line after a smeicolon (;) for style cohenrecy with lines 36 and 46 (This is also bashate recommendations.)

The only issue reported by checkbashisms with the -f option is here and concerns double-brackets. [[ ] ] is not defined by POSIX

https://mywiki.wooledge.org/Bashism#Conditionals

$ checkbashisms -f kubernetes/contrib/tools/registry-initialize.sh
possible bashism in kubernetes/contrib/tools/registry-initialize.sh line 32 (alternative test command ([[ foo ]] should be [ foo ])):
[[ $(ls -A $BASEDIR/*tgz) ]] && echo "$BASEDIR valid" || exit "No chart package on $BASEDIR provided"



waht about this rather ? 

test -n "$(find $BASEDIR. -maxdepth 1 -name '*tgz' -printf "package found" -quit)" || exit "No chart package on $BASEDIR provided"
echo "$BASEDIR valid"

cf https://stackoverflow.com/questions/6363441/check-if-a-file-exists-with-wildcard-in-shell-script

indentation = 3ws here but was 2 before
(note bashate recommendation is 4 ws)
should be on the previous line after a semicolon (;) for style coherency with line 36 and  46
indentation is 1 ws here but was 2ws before
cleaned up values.yaml (runAsUser and fsGroup are no longer valid; also these gets set in common/_pod.tpl)
Had an indentation issue earlier; fixed and added this now

The role was necessary; without it had following error

   message: 'pods "chart-chartmuseum-64dd49bf5b-" is forbidden: error looking up
      service account onap/chart-chartmuseum-read: serviceaccount "chart-chartmuseum-read" not found'

I'm not really pleased with that (hardcoding is BAD ;P)

so here's my proposal


we know that the name of the secret will be {{ Release.name }}-chartmuseum-registrycred in namespace of onap

so we could have two options:
-n / --namespace --> give namespace (onap per default?)
-r / --release --> give the release name (no default? or onap per default?)

then we could do:
LOGIN=$(kubectl -n "$ONAP_NS" get secret "${ONAP_RELEASE}-chartmuseum-registrycred"  -o jsonpath='{.data.login}' | base64 -d)
PASSWORD=$(kubectl -n "$ONAP_NS" get secret "${ONAP_RELEASE}-chartmuseum-registrycred"  -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d)
helm repo add k8s-registry http://127.0.0.1:27017 --username "$LOGIN" --password "$PASSWORD"

let's keep as deployment for now (I want to perform basic tests to see what's really happening when updating) but we may move to statefulset (https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/persistent-volumes#deployments_vs_statefulsets)
please add a space between L18 and L19
so I guess the typo is "normal" ;) (as it's in both shell script and here ;) )
let's not set a value here (and automatically retrieve it in the shell script
:(. sorry, thought i fixed it - obv not everywhere..
I've incorporate this on recent patch and removed hardcoding
good catch.. fixed it (now only required in one place)
PS10 proposal seems good to me!
yep, this one is solved in latest patches

linelength > 120 char
